issues.title: fix(ui): restore action icon mapping + log opacity (closes #762)
issues.body:
## Summary
HIGH-priority regression fix for #762: most action-icon buttons rendered the magenta-checkerboard placeholder.

### Root cause (diagnosed, not guessed)
IconLoader lookup is **id-keyed** (correct -- not the fragility). The gap was pure **data**: 8 top-level actions the main action panel renders had **no entry** in `data/icon_mapping.json`, so `get_action_icon()` fell back to the placeholder. A headless probe confirmed exactly which ids: `operations, travel, financing, advertise, use_connections, interview_next, hire_best, onboard_next`.

These ids entered `data/actions/core.json` in **#627 (2026-07-13)** (operations/travel/financing) and **#664 (2026-07-17)** (the hiring pipeline) **without** matching icon mappings -- so they have shown placeholders since then. What changed *today* and surfaced it: the new office backdrop (**#760**) sits behind the action panel, making the placeholders newly conspicuous in Pip's playtest. `icon_mapping.json`, `icon_loader.gd`, and the mapped icons themselves were **not** altered by #715 or #751 (verified: those merges did not touch the mapping keys, the ids, or delete any assets). The other 10 core actions resolve to real icons.

### Fix
- **Mapped the 8 missing action ids** to existing repo icons (reuse is an established pattern -- e.g. `media_campaign`/`publicity` already share one). Entries appended to the `actions` block and marked `#762 interim` so the **art-batch lane (`fable/art-batch-649`)** can promote dedicated icons later without confusion. Did **not** add the batch's 8 new icons; edit is minimal/append-safe.
- **New fast-tier test** `tests/unit/test_action_icon_resolution.gd`: drives the same path the UI does (unlocked actions from `GameActions.get_all_actions()`) and asserts every id resolves to a real (non-placeholder) `res://` icon. This would have caught the regression.

### Log opacity (also in #762 scope)
The WATCH feed had **no background stylebox**, so its text floated over the office backdrop. `TerminalTheme.style_feed` now paints an off-black panel: `#170A1C @ 0.88 alpha`, matching the turn-counter box (`StyleBoxFlat_TurnCounter`) and backdrop scrim register.

### First-load grey (investigated)
Most consistent with a **one-time cold-import decode**: #760's WebP backdrops import on first launch after pull; the first frame can render before the `.ctex` upload completes -> grey, then cached fine after. A `preload()` would not help -- the scene's `ExtResource` already effectively preloads the texture; the timing is import-cache generation, not runtime lazy load. Resolved by the normal `--import` pass. Documented rather than adding churn.

### Tests
Fast gate: **539 tests, 0 failures, 60/60 files** (includes the new icon-resolution test).

Do not merge -- for Pip's review.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: feat(ui): office floor visual layer in WATCH -- walkers mirror staff count
issues.body:
## What

Integration **step 1** of the office-floor simulator into the real game (Pip's ask: "how far off is loading the office scene simulator with little walking agents into the game?"). Embeds the standalone `OfficeFloor` pure-view component into the **WATCH** screen as a small, dimmed office rendered under the feed, populated by walking employee sprites whose **COUNT mirrors the live staff count** -- hire a researcher, a walker appears; lose one, a walker leaves.

## Where it's wired

- **WatchScreen** (`watch_screen.gd`): instances `office_floor.tscn` in `_ready` (code-mounted like the existing filter buttons), tier 1, existing `artloop_char` frames, dimmed (`modulate` 0.82) and height-capped so it never fights the terminal feed. New `update_office_floor(state)` method.
- **main_ui** (`main_ui.gd`): calls `watch_screen.update_office_floor(state)` from `_on_game_state_updated` -- the existing per-update signal hook.
- **OfficeFloor** (`office_floor.gd`): new `snapshot_from_state_dict()` read-only adapter + `sprite_count()` accessor; floor/prop rendering from the promoted assets.

## Determinism isolation (ADR-0006)

Read path is `game_state_updated` (a serialized `GameState.to_dict()` Dictionary) -> `main_ui` -> `WatchScreen.update_office_floor` -> `OfficeFloor.snapshot_from_state_dict` (copies values only) -> `set_roster`. **Zero writes** to game state; wander uses the component's private cosmetic RNG, never the seeded sim stream. Documented in code. `appearance_id` is carried through the snapshot untouched as the seam for the Friday identity / portrait-variant-pool mapping (#758) -- no per-researcher appearance yet.

## Assets promoted (2026-07-21 pixellab re-roll sweep)

Into `godot/assets/office_floor/`: `tilesets/floor_concrete.png` (Wang base tile cropped + tiled as the floor) and `props/{water_cooler,filing_cabinet,server_cluster}.png` drawn feet-anchored at the landmark zones, tinted/dimmed. New-asset `.import` files committed; no unrelated import churn staged. Walker art stays on the existing `artloop_char` cast -- the character-cast swap belongs with the #758 identity decision.

## Tests

New `test_office_floor_watch_integration.gd`: walker count tracks staff across spawn / despawn / clear; the dict adapter maps the serialized shape (count, unmanaged flags, `appearance_id` passthrough) and degrades gracefully on missing/malformed data. **Fast gate: 545 tests, 0 failures.**

## Remaining steps to the full office sim (rough)

1. **Identity mapping (#758, Fri):** map `appearance_id` -> per-researcher pixellab cast + portrait; swap the walker art off `artloop_char`.
2. **Cosmetics:** hats/lab-coats presentation layer (assets already generated).
3. **Doom-reactive behaviors:** event-reaction hook (global signal -> sprites glance up on a doom spike), sky-swap in the window prop by doom tier, office-tier/moral-skew aesthetic (seams already present).
4. **Layout/polish:** real navmesh + authored office layout (Tier 2), phase-scaling to pods for Titan.

Do not merge -- Pip eyeballs it.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: art: icon round 2 -- evil laser cat, Dept of Documentation seals, dial refinement, P(doom|Bayes) logo studies
issues.body:
Round 2 of the icon/logo studies, implementing Pip's 2026-07-21 verdicts on PR #750. The generative set (gpt-image-1.5) was re-rolled per direction; the procedural set is retired.

## Laser-cat hunt
Pip's original laser-eyes cat reference is `godot/assets/cats/incoming/small-doom-cat.png`: a black cat, glowing amber laser eyes, glowing collar emblem, warm-red room. (The wider real-cat lineage lives in `godot/assets/cats/incoming/` -- web-doom-cat, arwen, chucky, luna, etc.; `web-doom-cat.jpg` is the painterly calico that seeded round-1's `gen_cat_face`.) `gen_cat_evil` channels the `small-doom-cat` menace: amber laser eyes + glowing collar + warm-red-to-aubergine ground, kept cozy-grimdark (no gore, no skulls).

## What's here (new `art_prompts/iconset_round2.yaml`)
Palette hexes sampled exactly from `docs/art/PALETTE_AND_DOOM_INTENSITY.md`.

| concept | id(s) | variants |
|---|---|---|
| Evil laser cat | `gen_cat_evil` | 4 |
| 8-bit doom cat | `gen_cat_doom_8bit` | 4 |
| Cat sitting / loaf (house restyle) | `gen_cat_sitting`, `gen_cat_loaf` | 3 each |
| Dept of Documentation seals | `gen_seal_dod_formal` / `_grunge` / `_wax` | 2 each |
| Doom dial (icon candidate) | `gen_dial_doom_arc`, `gen_dial_doom_semi` | 2 each |
| P(doom) dial-paren logo | `gen_type_pdoom`, `_inaction`, `_us` | 2 each |
| LaTeX typeset (local, no API) | `render_latex_pdoom_inaction`, `_bayes` | 1 each |

- Seal ring text is EXACTLY "DEPARTMENT OF DOCUMENTATION" (never "doom department"), hourglass centerpiece kept from `gen_seal_hourglass`.
- Doom dial color stops follow the catastrophe ramp `#F6A800 -> #E9752E -> #E24A3B -> #B31217`.
- Logo studies develop Pip's favorite `type_dial_paren`: the parenthesis doubles as the gauge, with Bayesian conditional notation. Two matplotlib mathtext (Computer Modern) renders are committed for typography comparison (`tools/assets/render_latex_pdoom.py`).

## Layout / pipeline
Images land in `art_generated/iconset_round2/v1/` (the `select_assets.py` gallery layout, force-added since that path is gitignored scratch). Only <=512px are committed; the 1024px masters are left unstaged (>1MB, git-history bloat -- regenerate from the YAML if masters are needed). API cost ~$1.80 (30 images, gpt-image-1.5).

Skipped per brief: [APPROVED] stamps, `type_mono`, wordmark font variations.

Do not merge -- review pass only.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Nomenclature + pacing: strategic-options unlock expressed at month grain; align fanfare hero
issues.body:
Pip 2026-07-21, playtesting the new backdrop build: 'dial in the strategic options unlock with that hero screen... game turns are counted through planning, and we want strategic models to kick in around turn 10 in the game, so that's nearly a year into the game on a month by month basis. Tightening up this nomenclature is important.'

Scope: (a) audit where strategic-options unlock timing is defined (turn counts vs months -- ADR-0009's layer-not-regrain means 'turn 10' may mean 10 planning phases ~= 10 months); express unlock pacing in MONTHS in data + docs; (b) align the fanfare_strategic_moves hero moment with the intended ~1-year-in beat; (c) this is a concrete instance of the turn/month nomenclature cleanup flagged in VOICE_AND_LEGIBILITY_SCAN (confusing-weird item 2). Ties: ADR-0009, #757 conditional-art notes. Size: S audit + ruling.

milestone.description: Public alpha readiness (distribution channel decision owed -- see docs/ROADMAP.md): first-contact UX fixes, onboarding phases 0-2, export metadata, share loop, leaderboard hardening, monthly league v0. Capacity basis: ~1-2 effort-days/week.

issues.body:
Pip's live playtest 2026-07-21 on current main (+#760): most action-icon buttons render the magenta-checkerboard placeholder; only safety audit and strategic show real icons. This is a REGRESSION from one of today's merges -- prime suspects: #715 (player-facing copy audit, hardcoded->variable renames could break name-keyed icon lookup) or #751 (ASCII purge touched godot/data/*.json -- check whether data/icon_mapping.json or action ids were altered). Diagnose by diffing icon_mapping.json keys vs current action ids and checking icon_loader.gd's lookup key. Note: 8 NEW icons are being promoted on the batch branch (fable/art-batch-649) -- coordinate so the fix and the promotion do not fight.

Also in this fix lane's scope (same playtest):
- Action log readability: raise the message-log panel background opacity slightly now the office backdrop sits behind it (log container is enhanced_message_log.gd / its panel in main.tscn).
- First-load grey: backdrop appeared grey/absent on first load, then loaded on subsequent loads -- likely first-import/decode hitch; investigate and note (preload or accept-with-comment).

Size: S-M, priority HIGH (visible regression).

issues.title: Session agenda: Friday 2026-07-24 (decisions + conversations queue)
issues.body:
Lossless capture of everything queued for Pip's Friday session, accumulated 2026-07-20/21. Check items off or re-schedule; this issue closes when drained.

CONVERSATIONS (design, ~20 min each):
- [ ] Doom-color spec: secondary/tertiary colour arrangements per doom level/band -- feeds the dial gradient (icon round 2), banner_doom_rising variants (held conservative pending this), and completes docs/art/PALETTE_AND_DOOM_INTENSITY.md. Pip: 'we can get pretty specific about this.'
- [ ] Fonts + wordmark: typography direction for the type_dial_paren logo (LaTeX/Bayes P(doom) studies incoming on icon round 2), in-game fonts (godot/assets/fonts/ is empty), wordmark refinement.
- [ ] Portrait system ratification: per-archetype VARIANT POOLS with random gender/appearance assignment (ruled directionally 2026-07-21; dossier-v2 set promoted as current consistent set, painterly kept as pool stock). Decide the assignment mechanism + how variants map to save/determinism (seeded assignment?).

DECISIONS (one-liners):
- [ ] Public-alpha distribution channel (docs/ROADMAP.md 'Decision owed' section: website-direct / itch / self-hosted web build)
- [ ] Art masters policy: Git LFS for 1024 masters vs 512/WebP-canonical (masters currently safe in G:/tmp/pdoom1-art-masters/)
- [ ] Rival roster naming pass (Pip's voice; WORLD_AND_LORE mismatch)

WORK WITH PIP IN THE LOOP:
- [ ] #735 leaderboard enable: real endpoint + token (Pip-only), hardening minimums
- [ ] Playtest the merged visual stack (theme #749, backdrop wiring #756-lane, icon promotions) in one build
- [ ] v0.11.0 release-notes voice pass (also fixes the bug-reporter overpromise)
- [ ] Grant: Thursday grantmaking.ai submission should be DONE before Friday -- if not, it is dead; Manifund page + Screen Australia Emerging Gamemakers (closes 27 Aug) next
- [ ] Icon round 2 + round 3 verdict passes in the upgraded gallery (evil laser cat, Dept of Documentation seals, dial, logo studies; icon/banner/portrait re-rolls)

Standing context: docs/strategy/SUCCESS_PREMORTEM_2026-07-20.md section 5 decisions log; DQ_INDEX.md for the design register.

issues.title: art: app-icon candidates + settings-bg alternatives (fast pass)
issues.body:
Fast-pass **stopgap** candidate set ahead of the afternoon designer hire -- quantity + variety over polish. Do not merge; this is for Pip's fast-pass iteration.

## Review here first
**Contact sheet: `tools/art_review/icon_pass_2026-07-21.html`** (open locally; relative image paths, click any icon for the 1024px master, small pair under each icon is the 32/48px render).

## Deliverable 1 -- app-icon candidates (15)
For the Windows-export application icon (SmartScreen fix, issue #732) + logo stopgap. All in the locked palette (`docs/art/PALETTE_AND_DOOM_INTENSITY.md`), rendered 32 -> 1024px so 32x32 legibility is checkable directly.
- **Office cat (3):** `cat_face`, `cat_face_doom` (soured-red worried variant), `cat_sitting`
- **Doom gauge/dial (4):** `dial_clock` (doomsday-clock), `dial_half`, `dial_ring`, `dial_radar`
- **Bureaucratic seal/stamp (4):** `seal_star`, `seal_hourglass` ("PAPERWORK THAT MIGHT / SAVE THE WORLD"), `seal_check`, `seal_red`
- **P(Doom) typographic (4):** `type_wordmark`, `type_amber`, `type_mono` (best at 32px), `type_dial_paren` (gauge inside the parens)

## Deliverable 2 -- settings background, 3 warmer alternatives (512x512)
Drop-in replacements for the sage/green `tex_painted_metal_panel_512` (the greenest kept ground), same peel + corner/edge-screw composition shifted warm: `warm_ochre`, `warm_terracotta` (warmest), `warm_bakelite` (cozy deep brown).

## Pipeline note / blocker
Intended pipeline was in-repo **gpt-image-1** (`tools/assets`), but **`OPENAI_API_KEY` was absent in every env scope** (no key file, no GEMINI key either), so it could not run. To still deliver real PNGs today without burning the parallel agent's pixellab credits on off-register pixel-art, these were generated **procedurally** (PIL/numpy, no API): `tools/art_review/gen_icon_candidates.py`, `tools/art_review/gen_settings_grounds.py`. Set `OPENAI_API_KEY` to run higher-fidelity gpt-image-1 versions (esp. the office cat, which benefits most from a generative model).

Spend: **$0.00**.

issues.title: Dev overlays (DEV MODE + F3) can't read live game state; 29 runtime errors; nudge/advance-turn wiring
issues.body: Playtest 85ad582: DEV MODE (backslash) and F3 both report **'no active game state'** during a live game, so risk-pools + rival-labs sections are empty. F3 also shows **29 runtime errors** (all 'color white refcounted ...') + a perf tab (RAM/FPS) but no risk pools. DEV MODE resource +/- **nudge buttons may not be wired** to change state (verify). **Advance-turn** gives no visible feedback (works? overlay may cover the UI). Also: **backslash should CLOSE** dev mode (toggle). Design guard needed: dev-mode state pokes should NOT corrupt the verification/event log.

issues.title: chore: docs typographic ASCII cleanup (em-dashes, box-drawing) -- low priority
issues.body:
Follow-up from PR #751 (blocking no-emoji hook + godot/ ASCII purge). Remaining out of scope there: ~1282 em-dashes and box-drawing architecture diagrams across ~79 docs/ files. Convert to ASCII (em-dash -> --, box-drawing -> |/+/- ASCII trees) in one mechanical PR, then consider extending the no-emoji hook's full-ASCII coverage to docs/ (excluding docs/archive/ and docs/game-design/WORKSHOP_2_BACKLOG.md, which remain exempt historical files unless separately ruled).

Ruled low priority by Pip 2026-07-21; build in a quiet window OUTSIDE active-lane sessions -- mass mechanical edits across docs conflict with in-flight doc lanes, so schedule when no doc-touching lanes are running.

Size: S (mechanical, scripted).

issues.title: diag+harden: leaderboard crash diagnostic builds + build-verify/scene-smoke/asset-validation defenses
issues.body:
## Two jobs: (A) prepare to NAIL the release-only leaderboard segfault, (B) bulletproof against the class.

**The bug:** RELEASE-ONLY segfault while LOADING the leaderboard scene (game-over -> leaderboard), BEFORE `_ready` runs. Headless cannot reproduce it (verified: the new smoke test add-to-trees + runs `_ready` on the leaderboard scene cleanly). So this PR ships diagnostic builds + protocol + defenses, not a blind fix. **Do not merge** -- Pip runs the builds first.

### Part A -- the kill kit
- **3 verified-fresh diagnostic builds** produced by `tools/build_release.py` (each proven built-from-current-source; see below). Placed at stable paths under `builds/leaderboard-diag/{debug01,strip01,min01}/` (gitignored).
- `docs/LEADERBOARD_CRASH_DIAGNOSIS.md` -- **committed** ordered kill protocol (which build, exact `--verbose` command, what each result means).
- `leaderboard_screen.gd` -- `[LBTRACE-n]` stderr probes bracketing every `_ready` step.
- `leaderboard_screen_minimal.tscn` + `leaderboard_screen_min_stub.gd` -- reproducible minimal-scene fixture for the structure-vs-content bisection.
- `theme_manager.gd` -- drop the two dead `tex_cyan_ispf`/`tex_oxidized_copper` string refs (nothing `load()`s them).

### Part B -- bulletproofing (half the value)
- **`tools/build_release.py`** -- exports from a clean state (`rm -rf .godot`) then PROVES the pack is fresh via a unique **marker filename** (key finding: script string literals do NOT survive as grep-able text in this project's binary-tokenized `.gdc` export -- the old "grep for a printerr tag" advice is unreliable; filenames in the pack file table do survive). No build ships unverified.
- **`test_ui_scene_ready_smoke.gd`** -- add-to-tree + run `_ready` on every `scenes/ui` scene + standalone screen (the existing instantiate-only smoke never ran `_ready`). 21 scenes exercised.
- **`tools/validate_assets.py`** + `validate_assets_probe.gd` -- load every asset under `godot/assets`, fail loud on any that will not resolve.
- **`docs/POSTMORTEM_v0.11.0_ALPHA.md`** -- stale-export-cache trap, verify-the-pack discipline, the `all_resources` packing gotcha, and the headless render-gate limitation.

### Gate
Fast gate green: **534 tests, 0 failures (60/60 files)**. `build_release.py` dogfooded 3x (all PASS); `validate_assets.py` PASS.

### Best current hypothesis
Textures are almost certainly NOT the cause (dead string refs; welcome.tscn loads REAL textures fine as the boot scene). Leading suspect: the **`OptionButton` (SeedDropdown)** -- the one node type unique to this scene, which builds an internal PopupMenu/Window at instantiation, a plausible pre-`_ready` native-crash site in the release renderer. Build 3 (no OptionButton) is the direct test.

Headless cannot reproduce this class of crash; the human release-build playtest remains the final gate.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Art tooling: compare-and-contrast mode for kept assets (batch style reviews)
issues.body:
Pip 2026-07-21, from the round-2 sweep review: the hide-on-verdict viewer flow made clearing hundreds of cells easy ("way way way easier... I could now clear hundreds without getting tired"). Next capability: when doing batch STYLE reviews, an easy compare-and-contrast view of already-CHOSEN (kept) assets -- side-by-side across styles/palettes for refinement rounds, not just triage of new generations.

This edges toward a lightweight art-asset management layer (a CMS-ish view over art_source + verdicts.json). Keep it repo-resident HTML like the existing viewer (tools/art_review/build.py pattern), not an external service.

Not scheduled -- backlog until the next big style-refinement round makes it worth building.

issues.title: Check Godot port for team_player uncapped-stacking exploit + poaching rate (salvaged from pre-Godot notes)

milestone.title: v0.13 -- Rivals and News
milestone.description: Rivals M-tier surfaces (panel, race chart, poaching rework), News channel v1 (DQ-32), voice re-skin of generic event content, tutorial mode, DQ-22 ADR workshop.

issues.body:
**Salvaged from the pre-Godot PERSONNEL_BALANCING_NOTES.md during the 2026-07-16 archival sweep.** Two personnel-balance concerns from the pygame era — verify whether they survived into the Godot port, fix if present. For the QA/bug-shakedown phase, not a blocker.

1. **team_player uncapped-stacking (potential exploit).** Old notes flagged a team_player trait whose synergy bonus stacked without a cap — N team-players compounding into a runaway surplus. By our exploit definition (engine misbehaviour, not clever strategy — DESIGN_PHILOSOPHY), an uncapped stack is a bug to get ahead of. Action: grep Godot researcher/trait code for additive-per-teammate synergy without a cap; if present, cap it + add a test. If traits are not ported yet, note so it is designed-out (ties DQ-15).

2. **Poaching rate.** Old notes had rival poaching ~4%/turn; Pip's target is roughly once a year (ADR-0011 / DQ-22 aggressive-hiring). Reconcile the current cadence to the once-a-year intuition (re-denominate like the other per-tick artifacts calibration caught).

Both feed DQ-15 (researcher archetypes) and DQ-22 (world-shoots-back midgame).

issues.body:
Context: #545 rewrites docs/sync-config.yml and sync-documentation.yml, removing dead no-op mappings. Today only docs/shared/* flows, and only into siblings' docs/ (dev-visible, not public/).

This issue: while #545 edits those files, add one real live mapping for the player guide.

Acceptance criteria:
- Mapping: SSOT player guide -> website public/ (player-visible), per the website-publish issue.
- Verify with a trivial change that the website receives it (no reintroduced dead mapping).
- Token choice (CROSS_REPO_TOKEN vs WEBSITE_SYNC_TOKEN) consistent with #545's consolidation decision.

Pointers: docs/sync-config.yml, .github/workflows/sync-documentation.yml, .github/workflows/sync-dev-blog.yml, issue #545. Size: S.
